children.text: complete the development of the collision avoidance system known as TCAS–II so that TCAS–II can operate under visual and instrument flight rules and can be upgraded to the performance standards applicable to the collision avoidance system known as TCAS–III;
develop and carry out a schedule for developing and certifying TCAS–II that will result in certification not later than ; andJune 30, 1989
submit to Congress monthly reports on the progress being made in developing and certifying TCAS–II.
a safe and orderly transition to the operation of a fleet of civil aircraft described in this subsection equipped with TCAS–II; or

children.heading: .—Operational Evaluation
children.label: c .—Operational Evaluation
children.text: Not later than , the Administrator shall establish a one-year program to collect and assess safety and operational information from civil aircraft equipped with TCAS–II for the operational evaluation of TCAS–II. The Administrator shall encourage foreign air carriers that operate civil aircraft equipped with TCAS–II to participate in the program.December 30, 1990

children.heading: .—Amending Schedule for Windshear Equipment
children.label: d .—Amending Schedule for Windshear Equipment
children.text: The Administrator shall consider the feasibility and desirability of amending the schedule for installing airborne low-altitude windshear equipment to make the schedule compatible with the schedule for installing TCAS–II.
The Administrator shall complete developing and certifying TCAS–III as soon as possible.
Necessary amounts may be appropriated from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund established under section 9502 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 () to carry out this subsection.26 U.S.C. 9502

children.heading: .—Installing and Using Transponders
children.label: f .—Installing and Using Transponders
children.text: The Administrator shall prescribe regulations requiring that, not later than , operating transponders with automatic altitude reporting capability be installed and used for aircraft operating in designated terminal airspace where radar service is provided for separation of aircraft. The Administrator may provide for access to that airspace (except terminal control areas and airport radar service areas) by nonequipped aircraft if the Administrator finds the access will not interfere with the normal traffic flow.December 30, 1990

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall require by regulation that, no later than , collision avoidance equipment be installed on each cargo aircraft with a maximum certificated takeoff weight in excess of 15,000 kilograms.December 31, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: a safe and orderly transition to the operation of a fleet of cargo aircraft equipped with collision avoidance equipment; or

children.label: B
children.text: other safety or public interest objectives.

children.label: A
children.text: cockpit-based collision detection and conflict resolution guidance, including display of traffic; and

children.label: B
children.text: a margin of safety of at least the same level as provided by the collision avoidance system known as TCAS–II.
